items.q: Which preposition expresses 'for' as duration (e.g., 'for two hours')?
items.why: Students choose para because it's the default 'for'. POR is used for duration/extent of time. Para 'for two hours' would suggest a deadline ('to be ready BY two hours' time').

items.q: How do you say 'I wash myself' in Spanish?
items.why: Students translate word-for-word. Spanish reflexive verbs use SPECIAL PRONOUNS before the verb: me, te, se, nos, os, se. The reflexive pronoun does the work English does with 'myself/yourself'.

items.q: How do you say 'I'm going to the beach' (talking to someone elsewhere) in Spanish?
items.why: Students confuse ir (to go) and venir (to come). IR = movement AWAY from speaker; VENIR = movement TOWARDS speaker. 'I'm going there' = voy; 'I'm coming here' = vengo. Critical for holidays/travel vocab.

items.q: Which is the imperfect of 'estudiar' for 'él'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: estudiaba. “estudia” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What is the university entrance exam called?
items.why: Correct answer: EvAU. “ESO” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Up to what age is compulsory schooling in Spain (end of ESO)?
items.why: The real figure is 16 years. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
